title: Catabrosa maritima
intro: Catabrosa maritima, commonly known as sea sandwort, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the coastal regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Catabrosa maritima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with sea sandwort include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from digestive issues to skin problems and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Catabrosa maritima is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It is further classified as the genus Catabrosa, which is a small group of plants. Within this genus, Catabrosa maritima is the only species, making it a monotypic genus. Catabrosa maritima is also known as fescue grass or sea pearl grass. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and its adaptation to coastal environments.
uses: Catabrosa maritima, also known as sea bent or sea bent grass, has been utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits.
